death.attack.living_unvanished.suffocate: %1$s suffocated in mud slurry while trying to escape %2$s
item.living_unvanished.mauritius_blue_pi: Mauritius Blue Pigeon Leftover Page I
Mauritius Blue Pigeon Leftover Page II
Mauritius Blue Pigeon Leftover Page III
leftover_page.living_unvanished.mauritiu:
At an illager settlement on a tropical island, we discovered the carcass of a bird unlike any previously known:
About the size of a pigeon, it had a silvery-white head and red tail feathers. Most striking of all was its beautiful indigo plumage, after which the biologist accompanying us provisionally named it the Mauritius blue pigeon.

Today I took several gentlemen to the island, where we shot quite a few blue pigeons. We divided the birds among ourselves and ate them, while the gentlemen took all the feathers.
Strangely, one of them even removed the seeds from the birds' stomachs to take with him, saying they came from some ancient plant. I will never understand these fine gentlemen...

More and more ships are intruding upon our island. The men aboard use fire-spitting iron rods to shoot the blue birds out of the forests, then carry them away by the heap on their great ships.
Now they can no longer be found even in the island's most secluded forests. I, too, am beginning to forget the sound of their calls...
leftover_page.living_unvanished.thylacin:
This new land is home to a species that closely resembles a wild dog, yet appears to be entirely distinct—it has tiger-like stripes, a hairless tail, and a pouch like a kangaroo's. As no literature has described it before, I have named it the ‘thylacine.’
These thylacines are often chased by packs of wild dogs, which steal their food and even prey upon them. Poor creatures.

That bespectacled lad actually told me that those tiger-striped wolves of mine do not eat sheep? What nonsense! How could there be a wolf in this world that does not eat sheep?
I must rally every hunter and rancher for miles around. We will wipe out these monsters that dare approach our pastures.

Notice
Due to a keeper's negligence, this zoo's most important exhibit—the rare thylacine—has died after prolonged exposure to the sun.
The staff responsible have been dismissed. By way of apology, we have decided that:

§bAdmission will be half price for one week starting today!§r
